examples.title: Energy-Efficient Network Request Batching
examples.description: Demonstrates batching multiple network requests to reduce energy consumption
examples.code:
// Network Request Batching Algorithm
// Reduces radio activation by grouping requests

PROCEDURE BatchNetworkRequests(requests)
 BEGIN
 IF requests.length > 0 THEN
 // Create a batch of requests
 batchedRequest = CREATE_EMPTY_REQUEST()
 
 FOR each request IN requests DO
 ADD_TO_BATCH(batchedRequest, request.data)
 END FOR
 
 // Send single combined request
 response = SEND_HTTP_POST(batchedRequest.url, batchedRequest.payload)
 
 // Distribute responses
 results = PARSE_RESPONSE(response)
 RETURN results
 ELSE
 RETURN empty
 END IF
END PROCEDURE

// Usage: Instead of 10 separate calls, make 1 call
dataRequests = [userData, settings, notifications, feed]
results = BatchNetworkRequests(dataRequests)
examples.explanation: Batching reduces the number of times the cellular radio needs to activate, significantly saving energy. Each radio activation consumes substantial power, so combining multiple requests into one reduces overall consumption.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for processing, O(1) network calls vs O(n)

examples.title: Energy-Aware Background Processing with JobScheduler
examples.description: Shows how to schedule energy-efficient background tasks
examples.code:
// Android JobScheduler Implementation
// Schedule work to run when device is optimal

CLASS EnergyAwareUploader
 BEGIN
 PROCEDURE scheduleUpload(filePath)
 BEGIN
 jobInfo = JobInfo.Builder(jobId, serviceComponent)
 .setRequiredNetworkType(NetworkType.UNMETERED) // Use WiFi
 .setRequiresCharging(true) // When charging
 .setRequiresDeviceIdle(true) // When idle
 .setPeriodic(24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) // Once per day
 .build()
 
 jobScheduler.schedule(jobInfo)
 END PROCEDURE
 
 PROCEDURE onRunJob()
 BEGIN
 // Perform upload when conditions are met
 uploadData()
 RETURN RESULT_SUCCESS
 END PROCEDURE
END CLASS

// Alternative: Use constraints for different priorities
PROCEDURE scheduleTask(task, isUrgent)
 BEGIN
 builder = JobInfo.Builder(taskId, serviceComponent)
 
 IF isUrgent THEN
 // Urgent: Can run on any network, not necessarily charging
 builder.setRequiredNetworkType(NetworkType.CONNECTED)
 ELSE
 // Non-urgent: Wait for optimal conditions
 builder.setRequiredNetworkType(NetworkType.UNMETERED)
 builder.setRequiresCharging(true)
 END IF
 
 jobScheduler.schedule(builder.build())
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: JobScheduler allows the system to optimize when background work executes. By specifying constraints like 'requires charging' or 'requires idle', we ensure tasks run during optimal power conditions, reducing battery drain.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) scheduling, depends on system scheduling

examples.title: Wakelock Management Pattern
examples.description: Proper management of wakelocks to prevent excessive battery drain
examples.code:
// Proper Wakelock Management
// Ensure wakelocks are acquired and released correctly

CLASS WakeLockManager
 BEGIN
 PRIVATE wakeLock = null
 PRIVATE isAcquired = false
 
 PROCEDURE acquireWakeLock(context)
 BEGIN
 IF NOT isAcquired THEN
 powerManager = context.getSystemService(Context.POWER_SERVICE)
 wakeLock = powerManager.newWakeLock(
 PowerManager.PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK,
 "MyApp:MyWakelockTag"
 )
 wakeLock.acquire(10 * 60 * 1000) // 10 minute timeout
 isAcquired = true
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
 
 PROCEDURE releaseWakeLock()
 BEGIN
 IF isAcquired AND wakeLock != null THEN
 IF wakeLock.isHeld() THEN
 wakeLock.release()
 END IF
 wakeLock = null
 isAcquired = false
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
 
 // Use try-finally to ensure release
 PROCEDURE performCriticalTask(context, task)
 BEGIN
 acquireWakeLock(context)
 TRY
 EXECUTE task
 FINALLY
 releaseWakeLock()
 END TRY
 END PROCEDURE
END CLASS

// Also use BatteryGuard pattern
PROCEDURE checkBeforeAcquiring(context)
 BEGIN
 batteryManager = context.getSystemService(Context.BATTERY_SERVICE)
 batteryLevel = batteryManager.getIntProperty(BatteryManager.BATTERY_PROPERTY_CAPACITY)
 
 IF batteryLevel > 20 THEN
 // Safe to acquire wakelock
 acquireWakeLock(context)
 ELSE
 // Defer task or skip
 LOG("Battery too low, skipping task")
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Proper wakelock management is crucial for battery life. Always acquire with timeout, release in finally blocks, check battery level before acquiring, and prefer alternative approaches like JobScheduler when possible.

examples.title: Sensor Energy Optimization
examples.description: Optimizing sensor usage to reduce energy consumption
examples.code:
// Sensor Management for Energy Efficiency
// Register, use, and unregister sensors properly

CLASS SensorManagerOptimized
 BEGIN
 PRIVATE sensorManager = null
 PRIVATE accelerometer = null
 PRIVATE lastUpdateTime = 0
 PRIVATE sensorDelay = SensorManager.SENSOR_DELAY_NORMAL
 
 PROCEDURE startSensorMonitoring(context, callback)
 BEGIN
 sensorManager = context.getSystemService(Context.SENSOR_SERVICE)
 accelerometer = sensorManager.getDefaultSensor(Sensor.TYPE_ACCELEROMETER)
 
 IF accelerometer != null THEN
 // Use UI-friendly delay for continuous monitoring
 sensorManager.registerListener(
 this, 
 accelerometer, 
 SensorManager.SENSOR_DELAY_UI
 )
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
 
 PROCEDURE onSensorChanged(event)
 BEGIN
 currentTime = System.currentTimeMillis()
 
 // Throttle processing to reduce CPU usage
 IF (currentTime - lastUpdateTime) > 100 THEN // 10 Hz max
 processSensorData(event)
 lastUpdateTime = currentTime
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
 
 PROCEDURE stopSensorMonitoring()
 BEGIN
 IF sensorManager != null AND accelerometer != null THEN
 sensorManager.unregisterListener(this)
 accelerometer = null
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
 
 // Batch sensor events for even better efficiency
 PROCEDURE startBatchedSensor(context)
 BEGIN
 sensorManager = context.getSystemService(Context.SENSOR_SERVICE)
 
 // Request batched delivery - more efficient
 sensorManager.registerListener(
 this,
 accelerometer,
 SensorManager.SENSOR_DELAY_NORMAL,
 0 // Batch period in microseconds
 )
 END PROCEDURE
END CLASS

// Optimization tips:
// 1. Use SENSOR_DELAY_GAME or SENSOR_DELAY_UI, avoid SENSOR_DELAY_FASTEST
// 2. Always unregister listeners when not needed
// 3. Batch sensor events when possible
// 4. Throttle processing in onSensorChanged
examples.explanation: Sensors, especially accelerometer and GPS, consume significant power. Key optimizations include using appropriate sampling rates, batching events, throttling processing, and always unregistering when done.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per sensor event with throttling
